creator: 000.fbsd@quip.cz
text:
I have KDE started from SDDM. I do not use console login to start X manually.

Thank you for your time and advice, I will report this issue to Audacious folks when time permits. Maybe I will have some spare time in September.

I have a wild guess that my original problem (global shortcuts not working) is related to this session problem. It seems in both cases KDE does not see Audacious as Xorg application running that's why KDE cannot send characters to Audacious and cannot save it to session for the next startup. But the whole Xorg is kind of magic to me, I am mainly  FreeBSD headless servers guy.
